judge.rationale: The candidate submission is written entirely in Dafny syntax, not Python. It is syntactically invalid as Python code and cannot be executed or imported as a Python module. The entry point BoundedCounter is not a Python class, there is no threading primitive (no threading.Lock or similar), no ValueError/OverflowError is raised at runtime, snapshot() does not return a copy of nonzero counts, and none of the functional requirements are actually implemented in the target language. The Dafny formal specification is interesting as a design artifact but completely fails to satisfy the task of delivering a working, thread-safe Python implementation.
